response:
To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a matrix $ A $, we compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $, where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $.

Given matrix:

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
-4 & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14
\end{pmatrix}
$$

We compute:

$$
A - \lambda I = \begin{pmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{pmatrix}
$$

Now compute the determinant:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = \begin{vmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

We compute this determinant using cofactor expansion or a calculator. The result is:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = -\lambda^3 + 10\lambda^2 + 11\lambda + 252
$$

Thus, the **characteristic polynomial** is:

$$
\boxed{-\lambda^3 + 10\lambda^2 + 11\lambda + 252}
$$
